results.possibleIdentity: Samuel M. Feldman was a prominent scientist and educator active in the late 1960s and early 1970s. He was a Professor of Psychology and Neuroscience at New York University (NYU) and also held research affiliations with the Department of Neuroradiology at the Albert Einstein College of Medicine, which is located in the Bronx, New York. His technical expertise was diverse, encompassing fields such as Bioengineering, Semiconductors, and Ultrasonics, particularly as they related to brain research and medical instrumentation. Feldman was a member of the Experiments in Art and Technology (E.A.T.) community, where he was recorded in the Technical Services index—an archive of engineers and scientists who volunteered to collaborate with artists. His index card in the E.A.T. records (preserved at the Fales Library at NYU) explicitly lists his employer as New York University and his technical interests as Bioengineering, Semiconductors, and Ultrasonics. He died in December 2006 after a long career as a teacher and researcher.
